projects.title: Layer-by-Layer Assembly of Nanosheet Heterostructures
projects.summary: Development of synthetic strategies for the exfoliation and controlled assembly of inorganic nanosheets into hybrid thin-film structures.
projects.detailedDescription: This project aims to study the basic chemical physics of 2D exfoliation. We are synthesizing new parent layered materials and using soft-chemical routes to produce stable colloid suspensions of single-layer sheets. We are developing layer-by-layer deposition techniques to build artificial superlattices with tailored magnetic and optical properties.
projects.scientific_motivation: How do structural confinement and artificial layering in nanosheet heterostructures influence optical bandgaps and magnetic super-exchange interactions?
research_objectives: Construct artificial superlattices via layer-by-layer robotic deposition
projects.expected_outcomes: Stable colloidal suspensions of monolayer nanosheets and artificial superlattice films with near-infrared luminescent response.

milestones.title: Synthesized parent calcium copper tetrasilicate crystals
Completed robot assembly calibration for LBL deposition

workflow.question: Can ancient pigments be exfoliated into high-luminescence 2D layers?
workflow.methods: Liquid-phase soft-chemical exfoliation using organic solvents.
workflow.characterization: Atomic Force Microscopy for layer height and STEM for lattice spacing.
workflow.results: Freestanding monolayers with highly stable near-infrared photoluminescence.
workflow.applications: Planar solar concentrators and high-security anti-counterfeiting ink.

projects.title: New Photocatalytic Systems for Solar Energy Conversion
projects.summary: Designing high-surface-area transition metal oxide materials optimized for hydrogen generation and solar light harvesting.
projects.detailedDescription: This research investigates how structural tuning and doping of metal oxides influences charge-carrier lifetime and catalytic efficiency. We are integrating exfoliated sheets into catalyst platforms to maximize accessible surface area and active catalytic sites.
projects.scientific_motivation: Can we stabilize threshold-switching currents and collective charge-density-wave oscillations at room temperature using quasi-1D van der Waals nanowire interconnects?
research_objectives: Grow high-quality 1T-TaS2 and TaSe3 crystals via chemical vapor transport
Characterize low-frequency electronic noise spectra across phase transitions
In situ heat and bias nanoribbons to verify structural changes
projects.expected_outcomes: Demonstration of a stable room-temperature current-controlled oscillator and topological interconnect nanowires.

milestones.title: Published noise spectroscopy analysis in Nano Letters
Observed real-time thermal phase stability via STEM

workflow.question: Can CDW transitions be stabilized at room temperature in low-dimensional devices?
workflow.methods: Device integration using graphene gates on hexagonal boron nitride.
workflow.characterization: Electronic noise spectroscopy and transport measurements.
workflow.results: Abrupt current switching and room temperature oscillatory behavior.
workflow.applications: Oscillatory logic gates and radiation-hard microelectronics.
